name: Falelua Stowers
ai_summary: Falelua lives in Provo, UT (15-20mi) and seeks FT/PT availability Monday-Friday. She describes a calm, relationship-focused approach to conflict resolution and values creating a supportive environment where children feel safe to learn and express themselves.

transcripts.q4: I live in Provo Utah. I'm willing to go ahead and travel anywhere between 15 to 20 miles around the area.
transcripts.q5: Um, I'm looking for full-time but I'm open to part-time just as well. My availability would be Monday through Friday. 9/26
transcripts.q6: So something like that happens. Our first priority would be making sure that everyone's safe. I'll step in right away and separate the kids. Check on the kid that got hurt. I'll stay calm in that time position and keep my tone really study. So this is drain situation. Doesn't escalate that all talk to each child one-on-one for a moment. I would like to understand what led up to it and help them put words to how they're feeling like if they were frustrated or angry especially if they don't quite have the language yet. But the child who hit I'm clear that hitting isn't okay. But I also focus on what they can do instead like using the words asking for help or just taking a break after that with things have cooled. Cool down a little bit. I'll bring them back together and gaudy quick conversation. I would encourage the child to take responsibility and apologize. And I hope in try help both of the kids move forward in a positive way. Overall, I would try to treat it as a learning moment, helping them building, better ways to handle big emotions and interact with each other.
transcripts.q7: I think I'd be a great apis up to teacher because I really enjoy working with children and help them feel safe. Heard and confident, I bring calm positive energy into the classroom, which I think could help create an environment where kids are comfortable learning and expressing themselves. I'm also very patient and flexible. I understand that every child, learns and communicates differently. So I try to meet them where they are in a just my approach to fit their needs, whether that's helping a child work through big emotions or friend, in a fun way to explain something new. I stay engaged and supportive. Another big thing for me is building strong relationships, not just with the kids, but with families and co-workers to, I think communication and Trust are really important. Aunt and creating a consistent and supportive experience for children over all. I care a lot about helping kids grow, both academically, and socially. And I'd bring that commitment and enthusiasm into up kid classroom every day.
